<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a>.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a>.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether to use persistent connections:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Mule uses persistent connections.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Mule closes the connection after the first request completes.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of connections to open to the backend. HTTP requests are sent in parallel over multiple connections. Setting this value too high can impact latency and consume additional resources without increasing throughput.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>When persistent connections are enabled, how long a connection can remain idle before Mule closes it.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If this value is <code>true</code>, Mule streams received responses.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Size of the buffer that stores the HTTP response, in bytes.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Database account url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Database account URL.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>All access key token that enables users to have full control of the Cosmos DB resources.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Version of the Cosmos DB REST service.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, the user must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [Create Document](#CreateDocument)
- [Create Permission](#CreatePermission)
- [Create Stored Procedure](#CreateStoredProcedure)
- [Delete Document](#DeleteDocument)
- [Delete Permission](#DeletePermission)
- [Delete Stored Procedure](#DeleteStoredProcedure)
- [Execute Stored Procedure](#ExecuteStoredProcedure)
- [Get Document](#GetDocument)
- [Get Permission](#GetPermission)
- [List Documents](#ListDocuments)
- [List Permissions](#ListPermissions)
- [List Stored Procedures](#ListStoredProcedures)
- [Query Documents](#QueryDocuments)
- [Replace Document](#ReplaceDocument)
- [Replace Permission](#ReplacePermission)
- [Replace Stored Procedure](#ReplaceStoredProcedure)

---

### Resource Token Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ResourceTokenConfig_ResourceTokenAuth">Resource Token Auth Connection Provider</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>AUTO</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Resource Token Auth Connection Provider

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a>.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a>.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether to use persistent connections:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Mule uses persistent connections.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Mule closes the connection after the first request completes.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of connections to open to the backend. HTTP requests are sent in parallel over multiple connections. Setting this value too high can impact latency and consume additional resources without increasing throughput.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>When persistent connections are enabled, how long a connection can remain idle before Mule closes it.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If this value is <code>true</code>, Mule streams received responses.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Size of the buffer that stores the HTTP response, in bytes.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Database account url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Database account URL.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Resource token.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Version of the Cosmos DB REST service.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>If HTTPS is configured as a protocol, the user must configure at least the keystore configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

## Create Document

`<azure-cosmos-db-connector-mule-4:create-document>`

Creates a new document in a collection. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/dbs/{dbId}/colls/{collId}/docs endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Db Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-generated name or ID of the database in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Coll Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the collection in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>X ms documentdb is upsert</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set to <code>true</code>, Cosmos DB does the following:</p><p>* Creates the document with the ID (and partition key value, if applicable), if the document doesnât exist. * Updates the document, if it exists.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X ms indexing directive</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>INCLUDE</p></li><li><p>EXCLUDE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The default for indexing behavior is determined by the automatic propertyâs value in the indexing policy for the collection.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X ms documentdb partitionkey</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Partition key value for the document to create. Must be specified only if the collection is created with a <strong>partitionKey</strong> definition, for example, ["partitionKey"].</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Key Token Config](#KeyTokenConfig)
- [Resource Token Config](#ResourceTokenConfig)

### Throws

- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNAUTHORIZED

## Create Permission

`<azure-cosmos-db-connector-mule-4:create-permission>`

Creates a new permission in a database. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/dbs/{dbId}/users/{userName}/permissions endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Db Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-generated name or ID of the database in which the permission is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the user that owns the permission.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>X ms documentdb expiry seconds</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Validity period of the resource token returned by the operation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Key Token Config](#KeyTokenConfig)

### Throws

- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNAUTHORIZED

## Create Stored Procedure

`<azure-cosmos-db-connector-mule-4:create-stored-procedure>`

Creates a new stored procedure in a collection. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/dbs/{dbId}/colls/{collId}/sprocs endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Db Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-generated name or ID of the database in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Coll Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the collection in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Key Token Config](#KeyTokenConfig)
- [Resource Token Config](#ResourceTokenConfig)

### Throws

- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNAUTHORIZED

## Delete Document

`<azure-cosmos-db-connector-mule-4:delete-document>`

Deletes an existing document from a collection. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/dbs/{dbId}/colls/{collId}/docs/{doc-id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Db Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-generated name or ID of the database in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Coll Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the collection in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Doc ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the document to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>X ms documentdb partitionkey</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Partition key value for the document to delete. Must be specified only if the collection is created with a <strong>partitionKey</strong> definition, for example, ["partitionKey"].</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Delete Permission

`<azure-cosmos-db-connector-mule-4:delete-permission>`

Deletes a permission from a database. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/dbs/{dbId}/users/{userName}/permissions/{permissionId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Db Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-generated name or ID of the database in which the permission is deleted.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the user that owns the permission.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Permission Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the permission to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CONNECTIVITY

## Delete Stored Procedure

`<azure-cosmos-db-connector-mule-4:delete-stored-procedure>`

Deletes an existing stored procedure from a collection. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request
to the /dbs/{dbId}/colls/{collId}/sprocs/{sprocId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Db Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-generated name or ID of the database in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Coll Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the collection in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sproc Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the stored procedure to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Key Token Config](#KeyTokenConfig)
- [Resource Token Config](#ResourceTokenConfig)

### Throws

- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNAUTHORIZED

## Execute Stored Procedure

`<azure-cosmos-db-connector-mule-4:execute-stored-procedure>`

Executes a stored procedure by performing a POST on a stored procedure resource. This operation
makes an HTTP POST request to the /dbs/{dbId}/colls/{collId}/sprocs/{sprocId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Db Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User generated name or ID of the database in which the stored procedure is executed.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Coll Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the collection in which the stored procedure is executed.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sproc Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the stored procedure to execute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Key Token Config](#KeyTokenConfig)
- [Resource Token Config](#ResourceTokenConfig)

### Throws

- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNAUTHORIZED

## Get Document

`<azure-cosmos-db-connector-mule-4:get-document>`

Retrieves a document by its partition key and document key. This operation makes an HTTP GET request
to the /dbs/{dbId}/colls/{collId}/docs/{doc-id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Db Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-generated name or ID of the database in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Coll Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the collection in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Doc ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the document to replace.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>If None Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether the operation is conditional, which means if the response includes a body, the value in the database is different from the specified value in the header. The value must be the ETag of the resource.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X ms documentdb partitionkey</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Partition key value for the document to read. Must be specified only if the collection is created with a <strong>partitionKey</strong> definition, for example, ["partitionKey"].</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X ms consistency level</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>STRONG</p></li><li><p>BOUNDED</p></li><li><p>SESSION</p></li><li><p>EVENTUAL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Consistency level override. The valid values are listed in order of strongest to weakest. The override must be the same or weaker than the accountâs configured consistency level.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X ms session token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>String token used with session level consistency. Clients must echo the latest read value of this header during read requests for session consistency.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Key Token Config](#KeyTokenConfig)
- [Resource Token Config](#ResourceTokenConfig)

### Throws

- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNAUTHORIZED

## Get Permission

`<azure-cosmos-db-connector-mule-4:get-permission>`

Gets a permission from the specified database. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/dbs/{dbId}/users/{userName}/permissions/{permissionId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Db Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-generated name or ID of the database in which the permission is retrieved.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the user that owns the permission.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Permission Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the permission to retrieve.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>X ms documentdb expiry seconds</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Validity period of the resource token returned by the operation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Key Token Config](#KeyTokenConfig)

### Throws

- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNAUTHORIZED

## List Documents

`<azure-cosmos-db-connector-mule-4:list-documents>`

Returns a list of documents under the collection. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/dbs/{dbId}/colls/{collId}/docs endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Db Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-generated name or ID of the database in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Coll Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the collection in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>X ms consistency level</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>STRONG</p></li><li><p>BOUNDED</p></li><li><p>SESSION</p></li><li><p>EVENTUAL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Consistency level override. The valid values are listed in order of strongest to weakest. The override must be the same or weaker than the accountâs configured consistency level.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X ms session token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>String token used with session level consistency. Clients must echo the latest read value of this header during read requests for session consistency.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>A im</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Must be set to <code>Incremental feed</code>, or omitted otherwise. Available for REST API version 2016-07-11 and later.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If None Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Available for REST API version 2016-07-11 and later.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>No header</p><div><p>Returns all changes from the beginning (collection creation).</p></div></li><li><p>"*"</p><div><p>Returns all new changes to data within the collection.</p></div></li><li><p>A collection ETag</p><div><p>Returns all changes made since that logical timestamp.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X ms documentdb partitionkeyrangeid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Partition key range ID for reading data. Available for REST API version 2016-07-11 and later.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Key Token Config](#KeyTokenConfig)
- [Resource Token Config](#ResourceTokenConfig)

### Throws

- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNAUTHORIZED

## List Permissions

`<azure-cosmos-db-connector-mule-4:list-permissions>`

Lists permissions of a user from a database. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/dbs/{dbId}/users/{userName}/permissions endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Db Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-generated name or ID of the database in which the permission is retrieved.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the user that owns the permission.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Key Token Config](#KeyTokenConfig)

### Throws

- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:UNAUTHORIZED

## List Stored Procedures

`<azure-cosmos-db-connector-mule-4:list-stored-procedures>`

Returns a list of the stored procedures in the collection. This operation makes an HTTP GET request
to the /dbs/{dbId}/colls/{collId}/sprocs endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Db Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-generated name or ID of the database in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Coll Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the collection in which the stored procedure is created.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Resource Token Config](#ResourceTokenConfig)

- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:NOT_FOUND

- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-COSMOS-DB-CONNECTOR-MULE-4:TIMEOUT
